Name: Mesón de Cándido
Tips: Spain's most famous cochinillo restaurant since 1884. Under the aqueduct. Plate-smashing ceremony. Book ahead in summer. Every president has eaten here.

Name: Restaurante José María
Tips: Critics' choice for best cochinillo in Segovia. More refined than Cándido. Book ahead. The serious local recommendation.

Name: Casa Duque
Tips: Cochinillo institution since 1895. Rustic stone interior. Good backup if Cándido and José María are full.

Name: El Fogón Sefardí
Address: C/ de la Judería Vieja 17 Segovia
Tips: Sephardic-influenced cuisine in restored medieval building in the Jewish Quarter. Completely different from the cochinillo restaurants. Atmospheric.

Name: La Concepción
Tips: Lighter option on the Plaza Mayor. Good for tapas ponche segoviano and coffee if skipping a full cochinillo lunch.
